title: Daily Extreme (station-local calendar day high/low)
description: STORAGE SCHEMA for the Phase 3.1 daily_extremes() rollup. One row per (station, local_date) describing the calendar-day temperature extremes derived from cached hourly METAR observations. Used as the resolution source for Polymarket weather markets in non-US locales (and as a parity sanity check for US settlements). Calendar day is in the station's IANA timezone (NOT UTC). Precision rules: whole-degree-C for international stations (round half-up to match issuer reporting); Tgroup-encoded display precision (0.1°C) for US stations — note that the underlying U.S. ASOS source precision is whole °F (≈0.56°C step on the °C grid), and tmin/tmax inherit the integer-°F lattice from the constituent observations. The 0.1°C rounding is informational; tmean_c is the one field where 0.1°C HALF_UP rounding adds value because averaging produces non-lattice values.

station.description: 4-letter ICAO identifier (e.g. "EGLL", "KNYC"). Daily extremes are computed per-ICAO; multi-airport cities (e.g. Paris LFPG vs LFPB) get separate rows that the per-event resolver picks between.

local_date.description: Calendar day in the station's IANA timezone (YYYY-MM-DD). NOT UTC — the station-local convention matches how Polymarket resolves NHIGH/NLOW-style markets internationally.

n_obs.description: Count of non-null hourly temperature observations contributing to this row. Daily aggregates with ``n_obs < 12`` are flagged ``low_coverage``: tmin/tmax/tmean are set to null and a WARNING is logged (not a hard failure — the row still ships so downstream consumers see the coverage gap explicitly).

tmin_c.description: Daily minimum temperature in Celsius. Null when ``n_obs < 12`` (low coverage). Precision: whole °C for international stations; for US stations the value inherits the integer-°F lattice (e.g. 10.0, 11.1, 12.2 from integer °F [50, 52, 54] via Tgroup recovery) — 0.1°C rounding is applied for stability but is a no-op on lattice values.

tmax_c.description: Daily maximum temperature in Celsius. Null when ``n_obs < 12``. Precision matches tmin_c: whole °C international, integer-°F lattice (0.1°C rounded for stability) for US.

tmean_c.description: Daily mean temperature in Celsius (simple arithmetic mean across all non-null hourly samples — NOT (tmin+tmax)/2). Null when ``n_obs < 12``. For US stations, averaging integer-°F-lattice °C values produces non-lattice means; 0.1°C HALF_UP rounding is applied here and IS meaningful (unlike tmin/tmax). For international stations, whole-°C HALF_UP rounding applies.

precip_inches.description: Daily accumulated precipitation in inches (sum of non-null hourly ``precip_1hr_inches``). Zero (not null) when the day had no rain but had observations.

source_tmin.description: Source identifier of the observation row that achieved the daily minimum (e.g. ``"iem"``, ``"awc"``, ``"ghcnh"``). Null when tmin_c is null.

source_tmax.description: Source identifier of the observation row that achieved the daily maximum. Null when tmax_c is null.

country.description: Informational: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code of the station (e.g. ``"US"``, ``"GB"``). Informational only — not used for join keys.

$comment: ARCHITECTURE DECISIONS: (1) Local-calendar-day key NOT UTC — matches how Polymarket and most international issuers resolve daily weather markets. (2) source_tmin / source_tmax preserve per-extreme provenance so a downstream consumer can tell whether a record-setting hour came from a primary METAR or a backfill PSV. (3) Coverage gate at n_obs<12 was chosen to mirror WMO's ≥1/day-as-significant rule but is intentionally permissive: half a day of obs is enough to flag a hot/cold spike but not enough to claim a reliable mean. (4) precip_inches is NOT nulled on low coverage — zero rain with two obs is still a valid ‘sum’ (it just means we only saw two zeros), and Polymarket precipitation contracts care about the total, not the sample count. (5) Whole-°C international rounding is round-half-up to match the issuer-side reporting convention (most non-US authorities publish whole °C in daily summaries); US stays at 0.1°C because that's what NWS CLI publishes. (6) PHASE 18: U.S. ASOS source data is integer-°F native (≈0.56°C step on the °C grid). For US stations, tmin/tmax taken as min/max of Tgroup-recovered observations are themselves on the integer-°F lattice; the 0.1°C rounding is a no-op on those values. tmean_c is the one US-station case where 0.1°C rounding adds value (averaging non-lattice values). Pre-Phase-18 code claimed '0.1°C precision' as if the source data carried independent 0.1°C resolution — corrected here.
